channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: Climate Stress Reshaping US Roofs, New Study Shows
item.description: Nearmap, a property intelligence provider, issued a report showing that chronic climate stress is accelerating roof deterioration across the nation The report, How climate stress is quietly reshaping America’s roofs, is based on data from more than 2.8 billion roof images captured in a historical aerial imagery archive. Areas previously considered moderate risk are increasingly experiencing climate conditions associated with accelerated roof wear, the study shows. The research shows relationships between climate conditions and roof longevity.

item.title: Probe Finds Deadly Florida Condo Collapse Happened Over Several Weeks
item.description: The deadly destruction of a Florida beachfront condominium actually started weeks before it collapsed into a pile of rubble in the middle of the night, killing 98 people in 2021, but the building had been vulnerable from the start, federal investigators found in a final report issued Monday. The combination of a structural design that did not meet building codes and alterations made over its 40 years meant the other parts of the pool deck weren’t strong enough to withstand the extra load, leading to the type of slow-motion collapse. “In the case of Champlain Towers South, these margins against failure were too narrow from the start.” The report underscores findings that have trickled in since the 12-story beachfront condominium catastrophe, which showcase weeks of building distress and deeper-seated problems.

item.title: Vessels, Cargo Valued at $125B Awaiting Gulf Passage: Allianz
item.description: Around 1,150 cargo-carrying ships with an estimated vessel and freight value of $125 billion and as many as 20,000 seafarers are waiting to resume operations in the Gulf after the Iran war shut the Strait of Hormuz, Allianz Research said on Wednesday. According to the estimate, cargo with a volume of 29 million GT is awaiting passage through the strait. “Even if the US and Iran agreement holds and the Strait of Hormuz is reopened properly, solid assurances of safe passage will be required, involving the international community, particularly if traffic is to return to its pre-war levels, up to as many as 140 vessels a day,” the Munich-based insurance group said.

item.title: Commodity traders offer £1m to crisis fund after FCA competition probe
item.description: Following an investigation, the FCA is concerned 11 traders may have hindered competition in commodity futures markets and infringed competition law by exchanging potentially sensitive information about their trading and/or coordinating their trading strategies with each other. We consider all competition concerns and, where appropriate, we investigate and take action.' The individuals under investigation were day traders in global commodity futures markets. The FCA is concerned 11 traders may have shared sensitive information or coordinated trades, potentially restricting competition.

item.title: Re-thinking regulation for the age of AI
item.description: But with the pace of change brought about by AI challenging many of the assumptions on which our markets and regulation were built, the question isn’t simply how we regulate for AI. Speech by Nikhil Rathi, FCA chief executive at techUK's Agents of Change: Generative and Agentic AI in Financial Services 2026. Speaker: Nikhil Rathi, chief executive Event: Agents of Change - AI in UK Financial Services 2026 Delivered: 24 June 2026 Note: This is a drafted speech and may differ from the delivered version On joining the FCA over five years ago, I said we would become as much a data and tech regulator as financial services regulator.

item.title: Why Ex-Meta CTO Mike Schroepfer Says It’s A Great Time To Build A Hard Tech Company: ‘Infrastructure Is The Moat’
item.description: The way you plan and test and iterate, and what you understand is very different.” In our conversation, we spoke about an array of topics, including energy as a major investment focus, his learnings from running Meta, why now is a great time to build a hard-tech company and what excites him about the SpaceX IPO. Mike Schroepfer founded Gigascale Capital after departing Meta as CTO in 2022. Key to starting the fund was Schroepfer’s experience building out the infrastructure to support Meta’s business.

item.title: Everest fixes on $630m of retro, as pricing falls again for new Kilimanjaro III Re cat bonds
item.description: Now, we’re told that the size targets have been fixed, with an aggregate amount of $630 million of retrocessional reinsurance limit now being sought be Everest from the two series issuance of Kilimanjaro III Re cat bonds. Everest now seeks $630 million of retro across the two Kilimanjaro Re III cat bond series that are being offered, in both three and four year formats, with aggregate and occurrence notes to cover impacts of major industry loss events named storms and earthquakes that impact the United States, Puerto Rico, U.S. Everest Group has fixed its target on an upsized $630 million of multi-peril collateralized North America focused retrocession from its new Kilimanjaro III Re Ltd.

item.title: Gothaer secures €100m Yardstick Re flood catastrophe bond at lower pricing
item.description: German insurer Gothaer Allgemeine Versicherungs AG has now secured its targeted €100 million of reinsurance limit to cover flood events in Germany through its debut catastrophe bond, as the Yardstick Re DAC (Series 2026-1) issuance has been priced below initial guidance, Artemis has learned. As we’ve explained before, Gothaer’s debut issuance, this Yardstick Re 2026-1 cat bond, marks the first catastrophe bond to ever come to market that is solely focused on flood risks in Germany. Initially, Gothaer was looking to secure a €100 million source of collateralized flood reinsurance protection from cat bond investors with this Yardstick Re DAC (Series 2026-1) issuance.

item.title: Noncorrelation, pure underwriting returns remain primary motivators for ILS investing: Gallagher Securities
item.description: The ability of insurance-linked securities investments to deliver a differentiated source of returns compared to broader capital market instruments remains primary in the list of motivations for investing into the asset class, analysis from Gallagher Securities found. Catastrophe bonds and similar insurance-linked securities (ILS) are seen as the main type of assets in focus and it’s no surprise given their properties match many of the motivators investors have for accessing returns from the insurance and reinsurance market. Gallagher Securities listed the main motivators that investors cited for accessing the asset class.

item.title: RGA names Senan O’Loughlin as EVP, Head of the Americas
item.description: Reinsurance Group of America, Incorporated, a global life and health reinsurer, has announced the appointment of Senan O’Loughlin as Executive Vice President, Head of the Americas, with the appointment taking effect on 22 June. The company noted that O’Loughlin joined RGA in 2021 and most recently held the position of Executive Vice President, Head of US Individual Markets. We are confident that Senan’s leadership, strategic vision, and global perspective will continue to drive growth and innovation across the Americas region.”

item.title: Allianz Commercial reports insurance pressures amid rising geopolitical risk and shifting maritime losses
item.description: Alongside geopolitical volatility, Allianz Commercial reports that the wider insurance picture is shaped by longer-term improvements in maritime safety performance. Allianz Commercial, the corporate insurance division of Allianz Group, has emphasised that marine insurers are operating in an increasingly complex environment as global shipping, responsible for around 90% of international trade by volume, faces heightened geopolitical and operational pressures. While marine insurance cover has remained available throughout the period, Allianz Commercial notes that premiums have risen, reflecting increased perceived risk.

item.title: HDI Global US names Shadi Albert as CEO as Jim Clark steps down
item.description: Corporate and specialty lines insurer, HDI Global (HDI), in alignment with its new strategy, Xcelerate29, has appointed Shadi Albert as Chief Executive Officer (CEO) of HDI Global US, effective July 13th, 2026. Jim Clark, CEO of HDI Global US, will step down effective immediately. Earlier in his career, Albert gained extensive experience across the insurance value chain, including senior roles at global brokers Aon, Marsh, and WTW, and in executive leadership positions at US-based insurance companies.

item.title: Rhode Island Opens Window to Civil Courts for Child Victims of Sexual Abuse
item.description: Rhode Island has joined more than two dozen other states that have enacted laws expanding access to civil courts for child victims of sexual abuse. Governor Dan McKee signed legislation amending the statute of limitations for child victim claims of sexual abuse and creating a two-year window for victims to bring otherwise time-barred claims against institutions and supervisors accused of enabling or covering up sexual abuse. They told lawmakers that not only does the legislation acknowledge the “realities of delayed disclosure and the long-term impacts” of child sexual abuse but it also “sends a strong message to would-be offenders and the institutions that enable them that they will be held accountable, thereby creating a safer environment for Rhode Island’s children now and into the future.”

item.title: Trouble at Surfside Began Weeks Before Deadly Condo Collapse, Final Report Finds
item.description: The deadly destruction of a Florida beachfront condominium actually started weeks before it collapsed into a pile of rubble in the middle of the night, killing 98 people on June 24, 2021, but the building had been vulnerable from the start, federal investigators found in a final report issued Monday. “In the case of Champlain Towers South, these margins against failure were too narrow from the start.” The report underscores findings that have trickled in since the 12-story beachfront condominium catastrophe, which showcase weeks of building distress and deeper-seated problems. Anatomy of a Collapse: Report Includes Video, Diagrams of Causes “In some locations, the design provided less than half of the code-required strength,” she said

item.title: Smoke From LA Warehouse Fire Affecting Residents, Businesses
item.description: Smoke continues to billow from a fire at a massive warehouse in Los Angeles, drawing complaints about air quality from residents and interrupting nearby businesses. A fire at a 491,000-square-foot cold storage warehouse located near homes in Boyle Heights continues to burn into day eight. Residents in the area have complained of respiratory symptoms, and local businesses have are feeling an economic impact from the ever-present haze, according to numerous media reports.

item.title: The False Economies in Insurance Claims
item.description: We did not write another polite industry memo suggesting “operational improvements.” We held up a mirror to the insurance industry and forced leadership to confront a reality many organizations have spent years rationalizing away: To quote another colleague, Heather Blevins: Claims “is the operational engine, the reputational foundation, and the financial heartbeat of every insurance organization.” Every premium dollar collected eventually encounters a moment of truth called a claim. Understaffing claims departments doesn't save money—it delays exponentially higher costs through reserve instability, litigation, and adverse development. Claims professionals, executives, reinsurers, brokers, regulators, and every person worried about the future of this industry — we need to talk.

item.title: Nationwide launches drone insurance for farms
item.description: Nationwide has introduced new insurance coverage options for agricultural drones. We’re proud to begin offering these new coverage options, which help give farmers confidence to incorporate drone technology into their operations while knowing they have protection in place if something goes wrong.” – Justin Owens, AVP of Farm Underwriting & Sales, Nationwide. The new offering combines property, liability, and aerial application coverage for drones used in activities such as crop monitoring, mapping, aerial imaging, spraying, and seeding.

item.title: Westfield Specialty acquires renewal rights for DOXA’s Arris Property Underwriters program
item.description: The post Westfield Specialty acquires renewal rights for DOXA’s Arris Property Underwriters program appeared first on Coverager - Insurance news and insights . Westfield Specialty has acquired the renewal rights for DOXA’s Arris Property Underwriters program, an E&S commercial property program that provides coverage solutions for real estate, hospitality, healthcare, education, municipalities, and manufacturing risks through wholesale distribution. The DOXA renewal rights acquisition is timely given the challenges the industry has seen with the property market over the past few years.” – Jack Kuhn, President of Westfield Specialty.

item.title: ReSource Pro completes acquisition of Flow Specialty’s AI technology and team
item.description: Resource Pro has acquired the technology, intellectual property, and core team of Flow Specialty , an AI-focused wholesale insurance brokerage. As part of the deal, Flow Specialty co-founder and CEO Sivan Iram will join ReSource Pro to lead its AI Factory, a dedicated unit focused on developing managed agentic services and solutions for insurance clients. The transaction brings 10 Flow Specialty employees to ReSource Pro, including co-founders Naor Rosenberg, who will serve as technical lead for the AI Factory, and Amit Ben Natan, who will lead product development for the unit.

item.title: 12 aseguradoras, entre las 200 empresas con mejor reputación de Merco
item.description: La 26ª edición de Merco Empresas, presentada ayer en Madrid, selecciona a las 200 empresas con mejor reputación en España. Entre ellas figuran 12 aseguradoras, una menos que el pasado año.

item.title: Cualquier mecanismo europeo de catástrofes debe complementar los sistemas nacionales existentes
item.description: Insurance Europe ha hecho pública su valoración del documento de debate elaborado conjuntamente por Eiopa y el Mecanismo Europeo de Estabilidad (ESM) sobre la gestión europea del riesgo de catástrofes naturales.

item.title: El 92% de los directivos en España sitúa la pérdida de datos como principal riesgo en 2026
item.description: El 92% de los administradores y directivos en España sitúa la pérdida de datos entre sus principales riesgos para 2026, 16 puntos por encima de la media global (76%), según recoge la encuesta ‘Global Directors and Officers 2026’, elaborada por Willis.

item.title: Driving an EV in These US States Saves the Most Money
item.description: Across the country, that number is fully tilted in favor of driving electric, but it varies by state. states, including Arizona, Utah and Washington – consumers pay more than average for gas and less than average for electricity, according to a Bloomberg analysis of fuel prices and household power rates. These are the sweet spots for EV adoption – only made sweeter since the Iran War spiked prices at the pump.

item.title: UK Tribunal Gives Go Ahead For $4B Lawsuit Against Apple Over iCloud Services
item.description: Britain’s competition tribunal has approved a £3 billion ($4 billion) lawsuit against Apple over its iCloud storage service, consumer group Which? filed the claim in November 2024 and argues Apple abused a dominant position by “trapping” users of iPhones and other devices into its iCloud storage service, limiting their ability to switch to rival cloud providers. The consumer group says Apple did this by technically restricting how certain files can be stored, tying iCloud to iOS devices and using prompts and system design to steer users towards its own service, weakening competition and driving up prices.

item.title: Supreme Court Ends Suit Alleging Cisco Helped China Pursue Falun Gong
item.description: Supreme Court further limited the reach of a federal law used to hold corporations liable for human rights abuses committed abroad, as it issued a ruling on Tuesday ending a lawsuit by members of the Falun Gong movement accusing Cisco Systems of facilitating religious persecution in China. The suit had alleged that Cisco knowingly developed technology that allowed China’s government to surveil and persecute Falun Gong members. The plaintiffs said China used the system to track and then torture Falun Gong members.

item.title: How to Improve Small Commercial Property Underwriting
item.description: Severe weather volatility and rising loss severity are placing sustained pressure on the small commercial property insurance landscape. Here, he advocates holistic, predictive approaches that integrate loss data, weather insights and granular property characteristics using advanced analytics and neural network-based models to improve risk selection and pricing and to support workflow automation that can be consistently applied at scale. Earlier research found that 68% of commercial properties are underinsured by at least 25% (based on 2020-2021 commercial property appraisal findings ).

item.title: Howden names Supaporn Chanin as power, energy director in Thailand
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Middle East conflict heralds ‘new maritime order’ for shipping: Allianz Commercial
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: GSIS sets aside US$1.1m in reserves for Mindanao earthquake claims: report
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: ILS managers, third-party investors backed 52% of Florida Citizens traditional reinsurance
item.description: Florida’s Citizens Property Insurance Corporation once again benefited from meaningful ILS manager and capital markets support for its traditional reinsurance needs in 2026, with these third-party capital investors taking 52% of the placement, while Nephila Capital was the largest single participant. Now, with Citizens having disclosed the final signed lines of its 2026 property catastrophe reinsurance placement with us, we can see that as ILS investment managers and other third-party investors took the majority for their strategies, the overall capital markets participation in the reinsurance tower is higher still. Which means ILS, cat bonds, collateralized and/or fronted third-party investor participations in Florida Citizens risk transfer and reinsurance tower for 2026, are providing around 88% of the total nearly $2.82 billion of limit.

item.title: Lower pricing sought for Fidelis Partnership Syndicate 3123’s $75m Woody Re 2026-1 cat bond
item.description: – Woody Re 2026-1 catastrophe bond issuance that is coming to market to provide industry-loss triggered multi-peril reinsurance to benefit the Fidelis Partnership linked Syndicate 3123 at Lloyd’s, is now targeted for lower pricing, Artemis can report. The Woody Re 2026-1 cat bond notes are designed to provide the Fidelis Partnership linked Syndicate 3123 at Lloyd’s with a source of catastrophe reinsurance against losses from named storms, earthquakes, severe thunderstorms, winter storms and wildfire events in part of North America on an annual aggregate, industry loss index trigger basis, over a three year term to the end of June 2029. It is the first catastrophe bond that will provide its protection to Syndicate 3123, the Lloyd’s structure launched in 2024 by the Fidelis Partnership in collaboration with capacity partner Pelagos Insurance Capital (formerly Fidelis Insurance) .

item.title: Karla Scott joins MSIG USA as Head of Cargo and Logistics
item.description: MSIG USA, the US insurance arm of global insurer MS&AD Insurance Group Holdings, is further strengthening its Marine platform and expanding its capabilities with the appointment of Karla Scott as Head of Cargo and Logistics. Leveraging her over 25 years of experience across marine cargo, logistics, inland marine, and hull underwriting, Scott will lead the strategic direction of MSIG USA’s Cargo and Logistics business. In her new position, Scott will report to Dennis Marvin, Head of Marine for MSIG USA, who commented: “Karla is one of the most respected leaders in the marine insurance industry.

item.title: Majesco points to accelerating AI adoption reshaping insurance operating models
item.description: For an industry like insurance—data-intensive, regulated, highly competitive and often challenged financially—the stakes and opportunities are high, but the potential business value and outcomes are even greater.” Majesco states that insurers continue to face a range of pressures, including rising operating costs, increasing risk exposure, talent shortages, ageing technology infrastructures and profitability challenges. Majesco, a provider of AI-native and cloud-native software solutions for the insurance industry, has released a new thought leadership report examining insurers’ strategic priorities for 2026. Titled Strategic Priorities 2026: The Frontier Insurer in the Intelligent Era of Insurance , the report is based on Majesco’s primary research into how insurers are approaching technology investment and business transformation.

item.title: Arch Insurance Australia appoints Fariha Neda as Northern Region Executive Assurance Underwriting Manager
item.description: Arch Insurance Australia (Arch), a subsidiary of Arch Capital Group and a provider of speciality insurance products and services, has appointed Fariha Neda as Northern Region Executive Assurance Underwriting Manager, effective immediately. In her role, Neda will oversee the underwriting activities across the northern region Executive Assurance portfolio, providing a range of liability solutions, including directors & officers, management liability, and financial institutions. Arch’s northern region encompasses New South Wales, Queensland, and the Australian Capital Territory.

item.title: Ship Insurers Set for Major Claims From Iran War, Allianz Says
item.description: The insurance industry is set to face significant claims for ships that were damaged during the Iran war, according to Allianz SE. “Clearly, loss of life and property damage – in terms of both vessels and their cargo – are the main causes to date,” said Regis Broudin, global head of marine claims at Allianz Commercial. The company said in its annual safety and shipping review that it had already received such claims stemming from the conflict, some of which could include total losses of vessels.

item.title: Aviva to Rebrand Lloyd’s Syndicate Probitas 1492 as ‘Aviva Syndicates’
item.description: Aviva, the UK-based insurer, announced it will rebrand Probitas 1492 as “Aviva Syndicates” marking a significant milestone in the integration of its Lloyd’s platform into Aviva’s Global Corporate & Specialty (GCS) business. The rebrand will see the syndicate operate fully under the Aviva brand from the end of September, when brokers and clients will see the “Aviva Syndicates” name and branding across communications, systems and market interactions, the insurer said. Aviva Syndicates will sharpen our presence in the Lloyd’s market and makes it easier for brokers and clients to access the full strength of our underwriting, risk expertise and global reach,” Storah added.

item.title: Recent Floods Show Michigan Residents are Vulnerable to Flood Insurance Gaps
item.description: Tom and Diane Peterman tried to buy flood insurance when they moved to their retirement home on the shores of Black Lake 14 years ago but were told it wasn’t available. Then came this spring’s historic and devastating floods across northern Michigan — in some areas, for the first time anyone can remember — swamping homes, pushing dams to the brink of failure and washing out roadways. The Federal Emergency Management Agency develops and updates maps that determine who’s in a flood plain and must buy insurance, and to help communities plan.

item.title: A major global broker in Australia moves commercial broker placement to API-first negotiation infrastructure
item.description: The post A major global broker in Australia moves commercial broker placement to API-first negotiation infrastructure appeared first on Coverager - Insurance news and insights . Worker compen s ation now live with four insurers; ISR, liability, and commercial motor due in Q3, bringing the panel to ten insurers A major global broker in Australia has moved commercial broker placement off email and insurer portals and onto API-first negotiation infrastructure, starting with workers compensation as the first of four commercial product lines, in one of the first Australian deployments of its kind. “This broker customer has moved that workflow onto purpose-built infrastructure: headless, API-first, version-controlled across both risk data and terms.

item.title: Colombia da el siguiente paso después del open finance, ahora va por los datos abiertos
item.description: La autoridad en regulación financiera en Colombia publicó sus recomendaciones para dar forma a la normativa sobre Open Data , un paso necesario para concretar el open finance, que fue decretado esquema obligatorio para las entidades vigiladas según el Decreto 0368 del 7 de abril de 2026 del Ministerio de Hacienda y Crédito Público . Según se lee en el documento, la Unidad de Proyección Normativa y Estudios de Regulación Financiera (URF) sugiere que seis sectores integren una plataforma única de datos abiertos: s ervicios públicos domiciliarios, telecomunicaciones, créditos con recursos propios, la Dirección de Impuestos y Aduanas Nacionales, el Ministerio de Salud y Protección Social y el Departamento Nacional de Planeación. Accede al análisis que impulsa la toma de decisiones en la banca latinoamericana Este contenido exclusivo está disponible solo para miembros registrados de iupana.

item.title: US$100bn cat bond market ‘in reach’ as 2026 heads for record issuance: Gallagher Re
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: ICA launches consultation on new legally enforceable code of practice draft
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Mutual of Omaha promotes Hobson to lead Advisor Network sales
item.description: Mutual of Omaha has promoted Nate Hobson to vice president of sales for its Advisor Network. Hobson joined Mutual of Omaha in 2007 and has held several leadership positions, including life and annuity specialist, sales director, managing director, and national sales director. Prior to joining Mutual of Omaha, he worked as a financial advisor at Morgan Stanley and MassMutual.

item.title: Portage Mutual Selects Akur8 for Pricing Modernization
item.description: (Image credit: IIR/Gemini.) Portage Mutual Insurance (Portage la Prairie, Manitoba), a Canadian mutual property/casualty insurer, has selected Akur8 (Paris) to accelerate pricing-model development across its personal lines portfolio. Portage Mutual will initially deploy Akur8’s pricing platform across its home and auto lines, with the potential to expand use of the system to additional lines of business, according to an Akur8 statement. “From the very first weeks of our pilot, Akur8 proved to be a genuine game-changer for our actuarial teams,” comments Ryan Cheung, Director of Actuarial Pricing, Portage Mutual.

item.title: American InsurTech Council Joins NCOIL Program
item.description: (Image source: American InsurTech Council and NCOIL.) The American InsurTech Council (Washington, D.C.) has joined the National Council of Insurance Legislators ’ (NCOIL) Corporate Institutional Partnership Program for 2026. “The National Council of Insurance Legislators welcomes the American InsurTech Council as a partner in the CIP Program,” comments Patrick Gilbert, Director of Policy, Administration, & Member Services, NCOIL. The American InsurTech Council, a nonprofit advocacy organization focused on insurance innovation and regulation, says participation in the program will support its engagement with state lawmakers on insurance technology, consumer protection, market competitiveness and related public-policy issues.

item.title: EU to Escalate Meta Probe Into Addictive Design That Hooks Kids
item.description: The European Union is set to ramp up an investigation into Meta Platforms Inc. The EU’s executive branch, the European Commission, is preparing to issue preliminary findings that accuse Meta’s Facebook and Instagram of using exploitative design techniques to keep young users hooked, according to people familiar with the matter. In the U.S., Meta and other social media giants face thousands of lawsuits over similar claims that its products are addictive and causing a mental health crisis among teenagers.

item.title: No Maps, No Insurance: Michigan Floods Expose Info Gap, Prep in Rural Areas
item.description: Then came this spring’s historic and devastating floods across northern Michigan — in some areas, for the first time anyone can remember — swamping homes, pushing dams to the brink of failure, and washing out roadways. But it hasn’t developed maps in many less-populated areas, including some Michigan counties that experienced unprecedented flooding. Their experience exposes vulnerabilities across the country, experts say, because flood plain maps don’t cover all areas.

item.title: Auto Safety Agency Opens Probe into Fatal Texas Tesla Crash
item.description: Avila, who was inside the residence.” Tesla Chief Executive Elon Musk questioned media coverage of the incident, saying on X that a Business Insider report about the investigation “makes no sense.” He added that Tesla’s Full Self-Driving system “drives slowly through neighborhood streets.” NHTSA typically opens more than 100 special crash investigations annually into emerging technologies and other potential auto safety issues. agency said on Monday it was investigating the June 19 crash of a Tesla Model 3 that was reportedly using an advanced driver assistance system when it struck a home in Katy, Texas, killing a 76-year-old woman. Ashok Elluswamy, who leads Tesla’s self-driving efforts, said on X that the driver overrode the vehicle’s automated system before the fatal crash in Texas.

item.title: Major Auto Theft Ring Targeting Municipal Airports Indicted in Colorado
item.description: A Colorado grand jury indicted eleven suspects in a widespread motor vehicle theft ring that targeted vehicles parked at small municipal airports along the Front Range between July 2024 and January 2025, according to a joint press release issued by Attorney General Phil Weiser and Denver District Attorney John Walsh yesterday. The value of the 41 stolen vehicles, stolen property, and property damage carried out by the criminal motor vehicle theft ring exceeds $900,000. “I am grateful to DA Walsh and other law enforcement partners who helped take down this latest motor vehicle theft crime ring that harmed many victims and communities along the Front Range.

item.title: Anthropic Backer Menlo Ventures Raises $3B In New Funds To Back AI Startups Across Stages
item.description: The firm’s new capital is across two funds: Menlo Ventures XVII , earmarked for seed and Series A startups, and Menlo Inflection IV , a growth fund for Series B and later startups that are “already pulling away from the pack and on their way to becoming the breakout names of the AI era.” Along with Anthropic, other notable Menlo Ventures investments over the years include Uber , Roku , Warby Parker , Siri and Gilead Sciences . Venture investor Menlo Ventures 1 said Tuesday that it has raised $3 billion in new capital — the largest new raise in the firm’s 50-year history — to back AI-focused startups across enterprise, healthcare and consumer sectors. While Menlo Ventures’ investment in Anthropic’s Series C round was not its first bet on artificial intelligence, the firm described it as its “flag-planting moment.” “We made our first investment in Anthropic in 2023, when the company was pre-product, pre-revenue.

item.title: Porch sponsoring debut $100m Harbor Crest Re 2026-1 catastrophe bond
item.description: Porch Group, the US homeowners specialist insurer, has entered the catastrophe bond market for the first time, aiming to secure $100 million in multi-peril collateralized catastrophe reinsurance through a debut Harbor Crest Re Ltd. It’s good to see another first time sponsor enter the cat bond market in 2026 and in Porch it is an expansive company that over-time may benefit from utilising more catastrophe bond coverage to augment and diversify its sources of reinsurance capital. Porch Group underwrites its homeowners insurance through the Porch Insurance Reciprocal Exchange and its Homeowners of America Insurance Company, both of which are set to be beneficiaries to its first venture into the catastrophe bond market for reinsurance protection.
